functions.notes: Fail-closed default (no ruleset loaded -> Deny): verified. SNG RuleCompiler emitted a default-deny nftables ruleset; the Linux kernel nft parser accepts it (`nft -c -f -` exit 0) — the rendered enforcement artifact is syntactically and semantically valid.

cases.description: telnet to internal host (legacy, lateral movement)

functions.notes: Kernel nftables enforcement matches the userspace engine on all 12 corpus flows: the SNG-rendered ruleset was installed in a network namespace and each flow's verdict was read back from the kernel forward path (veth + counter). No kernel/userspace divergence.

cases.description: telnet to internal host (legacy, lateral movement)

functions.notes: Real ExtAuthzHandler categorize->deny-list path. Forbidden categories (malware/phishing/gambling/adult) blocked; sanctioned + uncategorized traffic permitted.

features.name: Session revocation (step 0)
features.how: Before any other check, the device-id and user-id are tested against an ArcSwap revocation set the control plane pushes over NATS; a hit denies immediately — enabling instant cut-off on compromise or offboarding without waiting for posture/MFA TTLs to expire.

features.name: Tenant isolation guard
features.how: The request's app/device/identity must all resolve within the policy's tenant; a cross-tenant mismatch is denied before any entitlement logic runs.
features.coverage: Hard tenant boundary on every request

features.name: Geo / time / network conditions
features.how: Optional per-app AccessConditions check the request's GeoIP country against allow/block lists, the source network class (corporate/vpn/public), and a UTC TimeWindow (hours + days-of-week) carried in the signed bundle.

features.name: Tag / label conditions
features.how: App, device, and user carry key=value tag maps; per-app TagConditions assert Equals/NotEquals/Exists/NotExists against them (e.g. device managed=true, user risk_tier=elevated) as policy-driven gates with no connector required.
features.coverage: Foundation for attribute-based access on bundle-delivered tags

features.name: Group entitlement
features.how: The resolved identity's group memberships must satisfy the app's required groups before access is granted.

features.name: Risk-adaptive posture
features.how: Device posture is scored 0-100 from weighted signals (disk encryption 25, OS patch 25, anti-malware 20, firewall 15, screen-lock 15) and compared to a per-app min-score threshold (None/Basic/Strict map to 0/60/90), replacing the old 3-level bucket with any-granularity thresholds.

features.name: Expanded posture hard gates
features.how: Beyond the weighted score, an app may declare independent hard gates on the expanded posture signals: require_edr (the EDR sensor must report healthy), min_patch_days (the OS must have been patched within N days), and max_av_definition_age_hours (antivirus must be enabled with definitions no older than N hours). Each is enforced separately from the score, so a device with a perfect score is still denied if its EDR was killed, its patches lapsed, or its AV signatures went stale — and the continuous re-evaluation loop revokes live sessions the moment a pushed posture trips any gate.
features.coverage: Reason `DevicePostureInsufficient` on degraded EDR / stale AV / out-of-date patch

features.name: Per-app MFA freshness override
features.how: MFA recency is enforced against the policy-global max-age unless the app sets an mfa_max_age_override_ms, letting a high-risk app demand MFA every 30 min while low-risk apps stay at the 8 h default.

functions.notes: Real ZtnaService.evaluate brokering. Denies unknown app/device/identity, stale posture, insufficient posture, degraded EDR, stale AV definitions, out-of-date OS patches, stale MFA, missing entitlement, and cross-tenant requests; admits authorized engineers on compliant devices. With the opt-in subjectless_degraded_eval flag on, a subjectless request runs every device+context+posture gate and fails closed with an explicit IdentityAbsent deny when they pass, and still denies on a genuine device failure.

cases.description: insufficient posture (only disk encryption on, below Basic score floor)
engineer, pristine device, fresh MFA -> crm
sales user -> low-risk wiki (no posture floor)
degraded eval: subjectless request, device+context gates pass -> IdentityAbsent deny
degraded eval: subjectless request never masks a device failure (stale posture -> deny)

features.name: Check-digit validators
features.how: Each national-ID regex match is confirmed by its statutory check-digit algorithm (ISO 7064 Mod 11-2, weighted mod-11, Luhn, Verhoeff, per-series tables) plus date/prefix invariants; a pass boosts confidence to 1.0, a fail suppresses the match — this is what keeps the false-positive rate at 0%.
features.coverage: 24 validated detectors: China, Japan, Korea, Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, India (Aadhaar+PAN), UAE, Saudi, Kuwait, plus the WS5 breadth — UK (NINO+NHS), Canada SIN, Australia (TFN+Medicare), Germany Personalausweis, France INSEE, Brazil (CPF+CNPJ), EU (IBAN+VAT), Philippines UMID, Indonesia NIK

features.name: Proximity context analysis
features.how: An Aho-Corasick automaton scans a window around each match for per-locale context keywords (e.g. 身份证, マイナンバー, آधার, emirates id); a hit raises confidence (+0.15) and counter-context (test/sample/example) lowers it (-0.30).
features.coverage: Per-locale keyword dictionaries for CN/JP/IN/AE/SA, used for detectors without a check digit (Qatar QID, Bahrain CPR)

features.name: Unicode normalization + CJK/Thai tokenization
features.how: Text is NFC-normalized and Unicode case-folded before matching (handles Arabic diacritics and full/half-width CJK); SimHash fingerprints segment CJK into character bigrams and Thai into trigrams instead of whitespace tokens.
features.coverage: Byte-for-byte synchronized Rust + Go normalization paths

features.name: Regional compliance templates
features.how: Pre-built rule bundles bind the validated detectors to a jurisdiction's PII regime and an enforcement action (block/redact), so an operator enables a regime in one click rather than wiring individual patterns.
features.coverage: PIPL, APPI, PIPA, PDPA (SG/TH), India PII, Malaysia PII, PDPL (SA), GCC PII

functions.notes: Real ContentClassifier over generated Asia + GCC national-ID corpora. Valid identifiers (correct check digit) must be detected; same-length identifiers with a wrong check digit must be suppressed by the validators.

features.name: On-device ONNX NER inference
features.how: A multinomial logistic-regression head (MatMul → Add → Softmax) runs per token over a 16+ dimensional deterministic feature vector via the ort (ONNX Runtime) crate; argmax above a 0.60 confidence threshold labels the token and consecutive same-class tokens merge into one entity span.
features.coverage: person_name, address, phone_number, bank_account, medical_record, legal_document, medical_record_number, driver_license, tax_id, date_of_birth, passport_number, national_id

features.name: Deterministic feature extraction
features.how: Token shape (title-case, digit ratio, phone/account/code shapes), a ±2 context-keyword window (name titles, address/phone/bank/medical/legal cues), and a common-name gazetteer; the Rust featurizer is pinned byte-for-byte to the Python exporter by a featurecheck fixture.
features.coverage: 16+ feature dimensions, no embedding table — fully explainable

features.name: Signed-model trust chain + regex fail-safe
features.how: The model ships inside the Ed25519-signed endpoint policy bundle and is re-verified against the operator trust store before load; when no model is installed the engine falls back to a real regex+context NER so detection degrades safely rather than failing open.
features.coverage: ModelVerifier (same trust root as the policy) + RegexNerFallback

functions.notes: Real on-device ONNX NER (ner_v2.onnx) over a labelled PII corpus across all twelve entity classes, with benign and capitalised-non-name controls. catch_rate is recall; the precision field is tp/(tp+fp). Spec targets: precision > 0.90, recall > 0.85.

features.name: Signature scan engine
features.how: yara-x (pure-Rust YARA) compiles a signed rule bundle; scan() runs all rules over file bytes and returns typed matches with severity.
features.coverage: EICAR, PE/ELF binaries, JS obfuscation, Office macros, ransomware

features.name: Two-tier severity
features.how: malicious matches deny unconditionally; suspicious matches deny only under elevated-risk mode — mirroring the hash-based malware verdict.

functions.notes: Real YaraEngine::scan over the signed built-in rule set. Known-bad EICAR / PE / ELF / obfuscated-JS / macro-Office / ransomware payloads flagged; benign text, scripts, macro-free documents and PDFs pass clean.

features.name: Bloom-filter sinkhole
features.how: known-bad threat-feed domains are inserted into a sized Bloom filter; a query (and each parent suffix) is probed and sinkholed on a hit, with an allowlist override for business-critical infra.

features.name: Tunneling detection
features.how: stateless encoded-QNAME entropy/length check plus per-registrable-domain sliding-window query-volume and TXT-abuse counters.

functions.notes: Real ThreatIntelSinkhole Bloom matcher + TunnelingDetector. Known-bad feed domains (and their sub-domains) sinkholed with allowlist override; encoded-QNAME / query-volume / TXT-abuse tunneling flagged; ordinary resolver traffic resolves and stays silent.

cases.description: tunneling: query-volume flood to one domain
tunneling: moderately long but low-entropy name

functions.notes: Real Suricata 6.0.4 driven by SNG's ConfigGenerator-rendered suricata.yaml; EVE alerts normalised through sng_ips::EveAlert::to_ips_event. Offline PCAP replay in IDS mode.

features.name: Evasion-shape signature coverage
features.how: yara-x built-in rules extended with packer, archive-smuggling, HTML-smuggling, PDF-JS, VBA-obfuscation, encoded-PowerShell and eval-equivalent-JS signatures; scan() runs them over file bytes.
features.coverage: UPX, double-extension ZIP, base64 Blob HTML, polyglot PDF+JS, Chr()-built VBA, -EncodedCommand PowerShell, Function(atob())

features.name: Archive name inspection without unpacking
features.how: matches the plaintext ZIP local-file-header filename so a deflated payload's dangerous / double extension is caught without decompression.

functions.notes: Real YaraEngine::scan over an evasion-focused corpus (UPX packing, archive smuggling / double extensions, HTML smuggling, polyglot PDF+JS, obfuscated VBA, encoded PowerShell, eval-equivalent JS). Benign look-alikes (base64 data-URI pages, admin scripts, macro-free docs, clean PDFs/ZIPs) pass clean to bound the false-positive rate.

cases.description: VBA Auto_Open -> Shell statement form (no parentheses)
VBA Workbook_Open with Chr()-built string + CreateObject
benign page with base64 data-URI image (no decode/download)
benign page: base64 data-URI image + .click() (no decode/download chain)
benign static PDF (catalog only, no JavaScript)
benign PDF with comment, no actions
benign text mentioning UPX (no executable header)
benign VBA (no auto-exec, no process spawn)

features.name: Reassembly-resilient detection
features.how: Suricata defrag + TCP stream reassembly rebuild the payload before the content match, so a pattern split across IP fragments or one-byte segments is still caught.

features.name: Evasion-aware signatures
features.how: rules match the double-percent-encoded bytes and the DoH wire markers directly, catching evasions that survive a single decode pass.

functions.notes: Real Suricata 6.0.4 driven by SNG's ConfigGenerator-rendered suricata.yaml over an evasion corpus: fragmented HTTP and session-spliced TCP (caught after defrag + stream reassembly), double-encoded URIs and DoH tunneling (caught by evasion-aware signatures). EVE alerts normalised through sng_ips::EveAlert::to_ips_event; offline PCAP replay in IDS mode.

cases.description: fragmented HTTP directory traversal (/etc/passwd across IP fragments)
fragmented EICAR download (reassembled by defrag engine)
session-spliced SQLi UNION SELECT (one byte per TCP segment)
benign JSON API POST /api/query (DoH look-alike path)

features.name: Sustained-load wild scan
features.how: YARA signature scan over a noisy benign+malicious blend on a multi-thread worker pool

functions.notes: Real sng_swg::YaraEngine (built-in rules) over the noisy wild corpus under sustained concurrent load (8 worker threads, 6144 scans in 1607 ms). Verdict = block on ANY match (elevated-risk inspection). Includes evasive/novel-packed malware the signature set genuinely misses (honest false negatives) and benign-but-suspicious traffic it flags (honest false positives). Noisier proxy, NOT production traffic. Corpus: 2087 samples (457 malicious / 1630 benign ≈ 22% malicious; target 20%/80%), seed=0x53475744, sha256=07b2e837ed62, schema=sng-wild-corpus/v1. Generated by blog/harness/wildcorpus (deterministic).

False-positive-rate under maximum sustained concurrent load: YARA engine over the 1040 benign samples only (8 worker threads, 9728 scans in 2549 ms). No malicious cases by construction — catch-rate is not applicable (reported as 1.0 for a zero-bad corpus); the meaningful number is the false-positive-rate. Corpus: 2087 samples (457 malicious / 1630 benign ≈ 22% malicious; target 20%/80%), seed=0x53475744, sha256=07b2e837ed62, schema=sng-wild-corpus/v1. Generated by blog/harness/wildcorpus (deterministic).

features.name: Sustained-load wild scan
features.how: DLP content classification over a noisy benign+malicious blend on a multi-thread worker pool

functions.notes: Real sng_dlp::ContentClassifier (7 credential/card detectors) over the noisy wild corpus under sustained concurrent load (8 worker threads, 1204672 scans in 1500 ms). Malicious = content carrying a valid secret/card; benign = prose + near-miss tokens the structural validators must suppress. Noisier proxy, NOT production traffic. Corpus: 2087 samples (457 malicious / 1630 benign ≈ 22% malicious; target 20%/80%), seed=0x53475744, sha256=07b2e837ed62, schema=sng-wild-corpus/v1. Generated by blog/harness/wildcorpus (deterministic).

False-positive-rate under maximum sustained concurrent load: DLP engine over the 590 benign samples only (8 worker threads, 1964672 scans in 2500 ms). No malicious cases by construction — catch-rate is not applicable (reported as 1.0 for a zero-bad corpus); the meaningful number is the false-positive-rate. Corpus: 2087 samples (457 malicious / 1630 benign ≈ 22% malicious; target 20%/80%), seed=0x53475744, sha256=07b2e837ed62, schema=sng-wild-corpus/v1. Generated by blog/harness/wildcorpus (deterministic).

Real Suricata 6.0.4 (SNG ConfigGenerator-rendered suricata.yaml; EVE alerts normalised through sng_ips::EveAlert::to_ips_event) replaying the committed PCAP corpus under 8 concurrent Suricata processes (39 runs in 1227 ms, 3x replay per pcap). Detection-rate AND false-positive-rate measured under concurrent IDS load. Network-flow corpus is the committed PCAP set — smaller than the file/content wild lanes; noisier proxy, NOT production traffic.
